title: Opendoor Q1 2026 Earnings: Key Takeaways from Earnings Call
text: Opendoor demonstrated strong operational momentum in Q1 2026. The company purchased 2,474 homes, up 45% from Q4, while signing over 5,000 acquisition contracts—the highest quarterly volume since Q2 2022. Resale contribution margin improved to 4.4%, up 3.4 percentage points quarter-over-quarter, marking six consecutive months of improvement. Management expects to achieve adjusted EBITDA profitability on a 12-month forward basis starting in Q2, with Q2 itself guided to breakeven. The company reaffirmed its goal of becoming adjusted net income profitable by year-end 2026, regardless of macro conditions. Fixed operating expenses remained flat at $33 million in Q1 despite significantly higher transaction volumes. CEO Kasra Nejatian emphasized: The machine is now running and every day it runs, friction disappears and people move. Unrestricted cash reached $999 million, the highest in years, while aged inventory fell dramatically to 10% from 51% in just two quarters. The company launched Opendoor Mortgage in Colorado with attach rates exceeding expectations, with plans to expand to over 20 states by Q3. The Cash Now, More Later product now represents over one-third of acquisition contracts. Management highlighted AI integration across operations, noting tools that reduced buyer fall-through rates by double digits and cut pre-list renovation costs by 10-20% in pilot markets.
